nodes.context: Traditional organized crime syndicate in Sicily, structured around families.
Calabrian organized crime syndicate structured around 'ndrine (blood-related families).
Campanian organized crime syndicate structured around clans, less rooted in blood ties.
'Ndrangheta family from Platì, named after the Barbaro family.
'Ndrangheta family from Reggio Calabria, named after the De Stefano brothers.
'Ndrangheta family from San Luca, named after Antonio Pelle.
'Ndrangheta family from Gioia Tauro, named after Girolamo Piromalli.
'Ndrangheta family from Rosarno, named after Giuseppe Pesce.
Camorra clan from Marano di Napoli, named after the Nuvoletta brothers.
Camorra clan from San Giovanni a Teduccio, named after the Mazzarella family.
Camorra clan from Secondigliano, named after Paolo Di Lauro.
Camorra clan from Afragola, named after Gennaro Moccia.
Camorra clan from Forcella, named after Pio Vittorio Giuliano.
Camorra clan from Giugliano in Campania, named after Francesco Mallardo.

edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the traditional families of the Sicilian Mafia, other major Italian criminal organizations—particularly the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria and the Camorra in Campania, are also structured around criminal families.
edges.context: Both are major Italian criminal organizations structured around families.

edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the traditional families of the Sicilian Mafia, other major Italian criminal organizations—particularly the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria and the Camorra in Campania, are also structured around criminal families.
edges.context: Both are major Italian criminal organizations structured around families.

edges.evidence_quote: In addition to the traditional families of the Sicilian Mafia, other major Italian criminal organizations—particularly the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria and the Camorra in Campania, are also structured around criminal families.
edges.context: Both are major Italian criminal organizations structured around families.

edges.evidence_quote: Examples include the Barbaro 'ndrina, from Platì, named after the Barbaro family;
edges.context: Barbaro 'ndrina is a constituent family of the 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: the De Stefano 'ndrina, from Reggio Calabria, named after De Stefano brothers, Paolo, Giovanni, Giorgio and Orazio;
edges.context: De Stefano 'ndrina is a constituent family of the 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: the Pelle 'ndrina, from San Luca, named after Antonio Pelle;
edges.context: Pelle 'ndrina is a constituent family of the 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: the Piromalli 'ndrina, from Gioia Tauro, named after Girolamo Piromalli;
edges.context: Piromalli 'ndrina is a constituent family of the 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: the Pesce 'ndrina, from Rosarno, named after Giuseppe Pesce, among others.
edges.context: Pesce 'ndrina is a constituent family of the 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: Examples include the Nuvoletta clan, from Marano di Napoli, named after the Nuvoletta brothers: Lorenzo, Angelo and Ciro;
edges.context: Nuvoletta clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: the Mazzarella clan, from San Giovanni a Teduccio, named after the Mazzarella family;
edges.context: Mazzarella clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: the Di Lauro clan, from Secondigliano, named after Paolo Di Lauro;
edges.context: Di Lauro clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: the Moccia clan, from Afragola, named after Gennaro Moccia;
edges.context: Moccia clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: the Giuliano clan, from Forcella, named after Pio Vittorio Giuliano;
edges.context: Giuliano clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: the Mallardo clan, from Giugliano in Campania, named after Francesco Mallardo, among others.
edges.context: Mallardo clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.
